title: Common Production Pitfalls
concept: Synchronous API requests, direct database reads/writes, no log scrubbing, and missing circuit breakers.

exercises.prompt: What should be verified before deploying an AI agent into production?
exercises.explain: Before deploying, it's crucial to ensure that background queues are set up to manage tasks effectively.

cards.title: Introduction to Pitfalls
cards.body: In software production, mistakes can lead to major problems. Understanding common pitfalls helps prevent future issues.

cards.title: Synchronous API Requests
cards.body: Synchronous API requests wait for a response before moving on. This can slow down processes and cause delays.

cards.title: Waiting for Your Food
cards.body: Think of synchronous requests like waiting for your food at a restaurant. You can't order another dish until the first one arrives, which can take a long time!

cards.title: Direct Database Reads/Writes
cards.body: Directly reading or writing to databases without precautions can corrupt data or cause inconsistent application states.

cards.title: The Myth of Infinite Logging
cards.body: Some think logging everything is safe, but not managing logs, or 'log scrubbing', can overwhelm systems and create security risks.

cards.title: Circuit Breakers
cards.body: Circuit breakers help prevent system failure by stopping operations when errors occur.

right.text: Circuit open, operations paused due to errors.

exercises.prompt: What is a key reason to avoid synchronous API requests?

options.text: They save time by queuing requests
They can slow down processes and create delays

exercises.explain: Synchronous API requests can slow things down because the process has to wait for a response, which can lead to delays.

exercises.prompt: Direct database writes can lead to data corruption. True or False?
exercises.explain: True. Writing directly to databases without proper checks can indeed lead to data corruption.

exercises.prompt: Logs should be managed properly to avoid overwhelming systems with too much information. Without log ______, systems can face issues.
exercises.sentence: Logs should be managed properly to avoid overwhelming systems with too much information. Without log scrubbing, systems can face issues.
exercises.explain: Without log scrubbing, systems can become overwhelmed and face severe performance and security issues.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a common production pitfall?
exercises.explain: Direct database access without checks is a common pitfall because it can lead to data integrity problems.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes a major production pitfall that can lead to system failures?
exercises.explain: Using synchronous API requests is a major pitfall as it can create bottlenecks, slowing down the entire system due to waiting for responses.
